output.summary: Replace fixed-width Tailwind classes in FooterInfo with fluid flex-based sizing. Three changes: (1) right info group container loses lg:w-[640px] lg:shrink-0 in favor of lg:flex-[0_1_auto], (2) Hours/Contact/Social columns replace sm:w-48 with sm:flex-1 sm:min-w-36, (3) address items replace sm:w-[calc(50%-1rem)] with sm:flex-1 sm:min-w-48.

fileChanges.description: Replace fixed widths with fluid flex patterns in PHP frontend template
fileChanges.details: Line 69: Change 'lg:w-[640px] lg:shrink-0' to 'lg:flex-[0_1_auto]' on the right info group container. This keeps the container from growing (grow-0) but allows it to shrink if needed, sizing itself based on children's intrinsic widths rather than a hard 640px. Lines 71, 81, 91: Change 'sm:w-48' to 'sm:flex-1 sm:min-w-36' on each of Hours, Contact, Follow Us columns. This lets them grow to fill available space with a floor of 144px (close to original 192px but fluid). Line 54: Change 'sm:w-[calc(50%-1rem)]' to 'sm:flex-1 sm:min-w-48' on address items. With flex-wrap on the parent, this ensures 2 per row on desktop (each item needs minimum 192px, so with gap-8 two fit side by side in typical widths) while allowing fluid growth.

fileChanges.description: Mirror the same class changes in the TSX editor component to keep PHP and TSX in sync
fileChanges.details: Line 150: Change 'lg:w-[640px] lg:shrink-0' to 'lg:flex-[0_1_auto]'. Lines 152, 172, 200: Change 'sm:w-48' to 'sm:flex-1 sm:min-w-36'. Line 109: Change 'sm:w-[calc(50%-1rem)]' to 'sm:flex-1 sm:min-w-48'.

classChanges.oldClasses: flex flex-col sm:flex-row gap-8 lg:w-[640px] lg:shrink-0
classChanges.rationale: lg:flex-[0_1_auto] means grow-0, shrink-1, basis-auto. The container sizes itself based on its children's intrinsic widths (3 columns at min-w-36 + gaps) rather than a hard 640px. It will not grow beyond that but can shrink if the viewport is tight.
flex-1 lets columns share available space equally. min-w-36 (144px) prevents excessive shrinking while being more fluid than the fixed w-48 (192px).

classChanges.newClasses: text-xs font-medium leading-normal font-sans sm:flex-1 sm:min-w-48
classChanges.rationale: flex-1 + min-w-48 (192px) on flex-wrapped children ensures 2 per row when container is >= ~416px (2*192 + 32px gap). Items grow fluidly to fill remaining space rather than being capped at exactly 50%-1rem.

designDecisions.decision: Use lg:flex-[0_1_auto] instead of removing width entirely from right group
designDecisions.evidence: User requested flex-based sizing. Using flex shorthand with grow-0, shrink-1, basis-auto preserves the container's natural sizing behavior (based on children) while removing the hard 640px value.

designDecisions.decision: Use sm:min-w-36 (144px) as floor for info columns
designDecisions.evidence: Original sm:w-48 was 192px. A min-w of 144px (36 in Tailwind) provides a reasonable floor that prevents columns from becoming too narrow while allowing fluid growth. The flex-1 ensures equal distribution.

designDecisions.decision: Use sm:min-w-48 (192px) as floor for address items
designDecisions.evidence: Address items were previously calc(50%-1rem). min-w-48 ensures each item needs at least 192px, so with gap-8 (32px) and flex-wrap, two items per row on desktop is maintained naturally. Items will wrap to single column on narrow viewports.
